verses.text: Sl 105 Este hino louva a fidelidade do Senhor para com Abraão e seus descendentes em todas as jornadas — de Ur a Canaã (105.12), ao Egito (105.17,23), pelo deserto (105.37,41) e de volta a Canaã (105.44). Tudo isso cumpriu a promessa do Senhor a Abraão (105.9,42), base da própria existência de Israel. José, cujo sofrimento se transformou em glória, é paradigma de encorajamento para Israel (105.16-25). • Sl 105.1-15 (com Sl 96; 106.1,47-48) está incluído no cântico de louvor de Davi em 1Cr 16. 105.1-5 O poeta começa com a exortação “Dai graças” (cf. 106.1; 107.1; 118.1; 136.1). Os outros salmos seguem com o refrão “porque ele é bom!”. O restante do Sl 105 fala da bondade de Deus.

105.6-11 A história de Israel baseia-se no compromisso do Senhor com Abraão. Mesmo quando surge dúvida sobre o futuro da aliança de Deus com Davi (Sl 89), a aliança de Deus com Abraão permanece, e com ela o vínculo espiritual entre Deus e Israel, descendência de Abraão.

105.6-11 A história de Israel baseia-se no compromisso do Senhor com Abraão. Mesmo quando surge dúvida sobre o futuro da aliança de Deus com Davi (Sl 89), a aliança de Deus com Abraão permanece, e com ela o vínculo espiritual entre Deus e Israel, descendência de Abraão.
105.8 A aliança é o juramento irrevogável do Senhor de ser o Pai de Abraão e seus descendentes (Gn 12.1-9; 15.1-21).

105:9 Which covenant he made with Abraham,.... Or made it known unto him, and showed him his particular interest in it; promised that he would be his God, that he would bless him; and that in his seed, the Messiah, that should spring from him, all nations of the earth should be blessed, Gen 12:2, compare with this Luke 1:72. And his oath unto Isaac: he made known to Isaac the oath which he swore to Abraham, and promised to perform it, Gen 26:3, or concerning Isaac (a);

105:10 And confirmed the same unto Jacob for a law,.... The son of Isaac, and the grandson of Abraham, to whom it was renewed and confirmed, Gen 28:13. It is true of his posterity that go by his name, and even of all the spiritual Israel of God, to whom this covenant is confirmed and made sure: or "caused to stand" (b), as the word is; by the faithfulness of God by his oath annexed to his word, and by the death of his Son: when this is said to be "for a law", the meaning is, not as if this covenant had the nature of a law, as the covenant of works had;
105.11 Deus prometeu a terra de Canaã aos descendentes de Abraão como sua posse especial, onde viveriam como povo de Deus (105.44-45; Gn 12.7; 15.7-21).
105.12-15 O Senhor protegeu os patriarcas de Israel.
105.12-15 O Senhor protegeu os patriarcas de Israel.
105.12-15 O Senhor protegeu os patriarcas de Israel.
105.15 Abraão foi um dos profetas da nação (Gn 20.7).
105.16-22 Estes versículos contam a história de José (Gn 37–41).
105.16-22 Estes versículos contam a história de José (Gn 37–41).
105.16-22 Estes versículos contam a história de José (Gn 37–41).
105.19 testou o caráter de José: Gn 39.6-12.

105:20 The king sent and loosed him,.... That is, Pharaoh king of Egypt sent messengers to the prison, and ordered Joseph to be loosed, as soon as ever he heard the account which the chief butler gave of him, and of his skill in interpreting dreams. Even the ruler of the people; or "peoples": the subjects of Pharaoh's kingdom being very numerous. And let him go free, ordered his fetters to be taken off, and him to be set at liberty, to go where he pleased;

105:21 He made him lord of his house,.... That is, Pharaoh made Joseph lord chamberlain of his household; after he had him to court, and he had interpreted his dreams to him to his great satisfaction, and had given him such prudent advice to provide against the years of famine; see Gen 41:33. So Christ, after his resurrection, was received up into heaven, and was made and declared Lord and Christ, Lord of all, having all power in heaven and earth given to him;

105:22 To bind his princes at his pleasure,.... Not to lay them in prison, and bind them with fetters, as he had been bound; but to give laws unto them as he pleased, and bind and oblige them to observe them: for, according to his word, all the people of Egypt, high and low, rich and poor, were to be ruled; and, without his leave, no man was to lift up his hand or foot in all the land, Gen 41:40.
105.23-25 Estes versículos contam a história da aflição de Israel no Egito (Êx 1.1-14). 105.23 O Egito (hebraico *mitsrayim*) é chamado terra de Cam.
105.23-25 Estes versículos contam a história da aflição de Israel no Egito (Êx 1.1-14). 105.23 O Egito (hebraico *mitsrayim*) é chamado terra de Cam.

105.26-36 O salmista narra a história de Moisés e das pragas no Egito (Êx 7.20–12.30).

105.37-45 O Êxodo e a conquista de Canaã cumpriram a promessa de Deus a Abraão, Isaque e Jacó (105.9-10). Diferentemente do Sl 78, o poeta narra a bondade do Senhor sem comentar a história de rebelião de Israel (cf. Sl 106). 105.37 nenhum… tropeçou: o Senhor protegeu todo o seu povo (cf. 9.3).

105.37-45 O Êxodo e a conquista de Canaã cumpriram a promessa de Deus a Abraão, Isaque e Jacó (105.9-10). Diferentemente do Sl 78, o poeta narra a bondade do Senhor sem comentar a história de rebelião de Israel (cf. Sl 106). 105.37 nenhum… tropeçou: o Senhor protegeu todo o seu povo (cf. 9.3).
105.39 A presença do Senhor era tão evidente a todos quanto fogo nas trevas (Êx 13.22).

105:40 He opened the rock, and the waters gushed out,.... That is, God opened the rock, who before is said to spread the cloud, and bring the quails, for it was a miraculous affair; or Moses, by divine orders, which was done by smiting it, when waters flowed out in great abundance, sufficient to give drink to men and beasts, and which continued, Exo 17:6. This was typical of Christ the Rock, 1 Cor 10:4, and of the opening of his side, from whence flowed blood and water, John 19:34 and of his being smitten with the rod of justice, and by the law of Moses, from whom flow abundance of grace, and the blessings of it, freely to all his people;

105:41 For he remembered his holy promise,.... It was not owing to the goodness of this people, to their obedience to the divine will, to any worthiness or merit of theirs, that such signs and wonders were wrought for them in Egypt; and that they were brought out from thence in such a manner as they were; and were protected and so plentifully provided for in the wilderness; but it was owing to the grace and goodness of God, to his covenant and promise, which he sacredly and inviolably observed;
105.42-45 A história da redenção é também a história da fidelidade do Senhor.
105.42-45 A história da redenção é também a história da fidelidade do Senhor.
105.44 Canaã era terra de nações pagãs (111.6). O salmista pode também estar refletindo sobre a promessa de Deus a Abraão (Gn 24.60) quanto às terras dadas a Isaque (Gn 26.3).
105.45 O Senhor planejou que Israel testificasse dele pela obediência; Israel seria seu povo-modelo.
